QF.id: I Clearing Settlement and Risk Management_57

question.text: What is the position limit for a Trading Member (banks and non-banks) in USDINR contracts?

answer.text: Higher of 15% of the total open interest or USD 100 million
Higher of 6% of the total open interest or USD 20 million
Higher of 15% of the total open interest or USD 50 million
Higher of 15% of the total open interest or USD 1 billion

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The position limits table (Section 7.6A) specifies: "Trading Member (Banks and Non-Banks) in USDINR: Higher of 15% of the total open interest or USD 100 million." **The Trap:** USD 1 billion is specifically for bank TMs as authorised by RBI (not general TM limit). USD 50 million is for non-bank stock broker proprietary positions. USD 20 million is the domestic client limit. **Cross-Exam Context:** Four categories and their USDINR limits: TM (banks/non-banks) = USD 100M; Domestic clients/NRIs = USD 20M; Non-bank stock broker prop = USD 50M; Bank TM (RBI authorised) = USD 1 billion. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** USD 100M is the standard TM limit; the bank TM has a special higher limit of USD 1 billion when specifically authorised by RBI — a critical footnote distinction.
